p.em: Reference: Intel® 64 and IA-32 Architectures Software Developer’s Manual, Vol 3A

div.p: The PIC is programmed via Initialization Command Words (ICW) to shift the IRQ base offsets.
Master PIC: Base Offset 0x08 Slave PIC: Base Offset 0x70 Result: Hardware IRQs overlap with CPU Exceptions (0x00 - 0x1F). Example: IRQ0 (Timer) and Vector 0x08 (Double Fault) are indistinguishable.

g.text: ICW2 Master (0x20): Set Base Offset to 32
ICW2 Slave (0x28): Set Base Offset to 40

div.p: New Master Base: 0x20 (Vector 32) New Slave Base: 0x28 (Vector 40) Safe Range: IRQs map to 32-47, avoiding CPU Reserved space.
